pue_source: EcoDataCenter To Deploy World's First Chassis-Level Immersion Liquid Cooling
notes: TRIPLE MATCH: PUE 1.03, Iceotope chassis-level immersion cooling, ~2MW facility. World's first chassis-level immersion deployment. Climate-positive via district heating waste heat reuse. 14% CapEx savings, 90% rack cooling energy reduction.
Fjord-cooled mountain hall. PUE near 1.10 achieved. Offers DLC and immersion for highest densities. 8°C constant fjord water at 100m depth. Closed-loop titanium heat exchangers.
PUE 1.18, closed-loop water-free ring chilled water system. 54MW campus. 83% of CyrusOne 2023 new builds use water-free cooling. Frankfurt hub.

pue_source: Liquid vs Air Cooling for Data Centers 2026: Cost & Efficiency Analysis
notes: OCP-compliant Submer immersion deployment running 150kW racks at PUE 1.05. Completed Jan 2025. Competitive intelligence — Submer is a competitor.
DLC, PUE <1.2, waste heat to 8000 homes.
PUE 1.004. 16 patents. Competitor intel.
TRIPLE MATCH: Submer immersion, sub-1.1 PUE, 50kW.
DLC 100kW+/rack. PUE <1.2. 42MW. 1GW campus.
DTC + air. 130kW/rack. NVIDIA DGX-Ready.
PUE 1.15 at 70% load. 20.5MW. Adiabatic free cooling. Air-cooled — retrofit opportunity for liquid.
PUE 1.01 in hot humid Hong Kong. 40MW IT load. 252kW/rack. Two-phase immersion. Competitive intel — world benchmark.
PUE 1.1 via Air-to-Air evaporative cooling. No liquid yet — retrofit opportunity. 65+ MW across 8 Nordic DCs.
78MW hyperscale, PUE 1.2 (Amsterdam mandate). EUR 1B+ investment. Jan 2026 construction start. Single hyperscale customer.
PUE ~1.1. Seawater cooling via quarter-mile granite tunnel. Pioneering natural liquid cooling. Competitive intel for Google's approach.
120MW — world's largest liquid-cooled DC (2018). PUE 1.02. Two-phase immersion. Hot climate proof. COMPETITOR BENCHMARK.
40MW. PUE 1.03. Densest immersion DC at time of build (2015). COMPETITOR BENCHMARK.
TRIPLE MATCH: PUE 1.04, liquid cooling, 6.4MW. EU flagship HPC. Waste heat = 20% of Kajaani district heating.
12MW launched (expanding to 72MW+). PUE ≤1.2. DLC supported. 100% geothermal+hydro. Waste heat to agtech.
10MW full immersion. 368kW/rack. Tier III target. AI/GPUaaS. 15-year deal. Q4 2026 handover.
10MW full immersion. 368kW/rack. Mirror of Riyadh facility. Q4 2026.
1MW operational immersion in UAE. Edge/colo. Sharjah Research Park.
TRIPLE MATCH: PUE <1.05, 100% fanless DLC, 4.96MW. BullSequana XH3000. EU sovereign HPC. Warm water 40C.
6MW (qualifies!). Proprietary DUG Cool single-phase immersion. World's first carbon-free HPC campus target. 45-hectare site.
PUE <1.02 in TROPICAL Singapore via Firmus immersion. Extraordinary achievement in hot/humid climate.
PUE 1.1. 20MW. Liquid cooling up to 100kW/rack. Air-to-Air evaporative + DLC. 100% renewable hydro+wind.
PUE 1.015-1.02 WORLD RECORD. Research testbed. Partners: Meta, Ericsson, Vertiv, ABB.
36MW. DLC testing (LiquidStack immersion). Partial PUE 1.07 at Mitaka test.
20,736 Blackwell Ultra. $2B+. Asia's largest Tier IV. PUE <1.2. 2.25GW+ scalable.

address: Eldorado do Sul, Rio Grande do Sul
notes: 5GW authorization! PUE 1.2 (lowest LATAM). Zero-water liquid cooling. 100% renewable.
PUE 1.15! 400kW/rack DLC. Waterless. Merlin Properties REIT. Also expanding Lisbon.
PUE 1.09. Single-phase immersion. Chinese hyperscaler.
PUE 1.08. ML-controlled cooling. Chinese hyperscaler.
PUE 1.12 target. DLC+immersion. 600MW campus. India Tier 6.
PUE 1.16. World's first multi-storey adiabatic. DLC lab with JetCool. West Two £275M under construction with DLC+air.

Underground mine data center in Nordfjord, Norway. PUE 1.08-1.15 using fjord seawater cooling at 8C from 100m depth. Supports DLC, inline cooling, immersion, and CRAH. 80MW current capacity expanding to 200MW. 90MW cooling via 12x7.5MW heat exchangers. Up to 300kW/rack with DLC. Waste heat recovery program operational. 120,000 sqm whitespace potential.

PUE 1.07 via 100% free-air cooling at 66N latitude (avg 1.3C). 4MW facility on 2.5 hectares, 6 data halls. Acquired by Northern Data AG (2021) rebranded as Ardent. 100% hydroelectric power. GPU/HPC focused. Ardent states liquid cooling planned for future builds. Pod-based 180kW system (18 racks). Up to 25kW/rack.

PUE 1.10-1.15. Liquid cooling ready since 2023: in-row CDUs, in-rack CDUs, direct-to-chip, rear-door, immersion. Over 100kW/rack supported. District heating connected for waste heat. 100% renewable wind power. OCP Ready certified. Expanding from 24MW to 28MW (Jan 2026). Built 2024.

PUE 1.14 across 36-acre campus. 51MW IT load across 6 modular data centers. Hybrid cooling: free-air, evaporative (indirect), plus liquid cooling 'liquid optimisers'. 100% renewable energy + HVO backup generators. Rainwater harvesting for cooling. Tier III resilience. Operational since 2011. Synchronous replication to London.

PUE below 1.2 via fresh-air evaporative cooling. First London DC to deploy this cooling tech. 12MW total client power, 60,000 sqft. AI-Ready: supports direct-to-chip, immersive, and rear-door heat exchange liquid cooling within same workload. 6 data halls with flexible subdivision. Liquid cooled customer racks since 2015.

PUE 1.2 via ultra-free air cooling. 10MW capacity, 800 racks across 6 data halls. 30,000 sqft. Aqua EcoPro+ free cooling chiller units (3x500kW). Chilled water system with hot/cold aisle containment. No liquid cooling currently — potential retrofit opportunity. 15 miles from Central London.

4MW facility in Farnborough. PUE 1.25. 1,200 racks across 4 data halls (22,000 sqft). Up to 30kW/rack supported. Free cooling chillers. Hall 4 expansion added 380 racks + 1MW IT load. No liquid cooling currently — small facility with potential for upgrade. Enterprise clients (UBS). On same Cody Technology Park as Ark.

PUE 1.09 via seawater cooling (first in world for DC use, since 2009). Standard PUE 1.2 with chilled water, 1.09-1.1 with seawater. Saves USD 1M annually. Cooling-free over half the year. Part of Stockholm Data Parks. No IT liquid cooling (DTC/immersion) currently confirmed.

company: Boden Type DC / Boden Business Agency
facility_name: Boden Type Data Center One (BTDC One)
notes: WORLD-RECORD PUE: Category 2 PUE 1.025 (instantaneous <1.01). Pod 1 (OCP CPUs) PUE <1.03, Pod 3 (ASICs) PUE <1.003. 600kW total, 180 sqm whitespace. EU H2020 prototype. Patented EcoCooling evaporative system — no liquid cooling. ASHRAE compliant at -30C with <100W overhead for 140kW IT load. Hive Digital partnership.

PUE 1.2 via award-winning evaporative cooling. 2MW capacity, near full. 150,000 sqft across 3 data floors. ~400 cabinets at 4kW/rack. 2N UPS, N+1 generators. No liquid cooling — potential small-facility retrofit opportunity. 100% uptime record.

PUE 1.2 design target. 55MW compute capacity across 6 modular DCs. Mix of direct air, indirect air, and liquid cooling. SQ17 building: 24 data halls at 0.5MW each (12MW total). P1-P3: 6 data rooms at 760kW each (182 cabinets at 4.2kW). Multiple 33kV feeds. 100% renewable energy. Operational since 2010. Supports AI/high-density workloads.

PUE 1.1242 (TUV SUD certified — best result TUV ever recorded). Free air cooling only, no liquid cooling currently. Qualifies on PUE alone. Large facility, not a cooling lead but demonstrates market benchmark.

TRIPLE MATCH: PUE 1.05, Diggers direct liquid cooling (cold box with aluminum cold plates), 6MW connected load (A1 NGDC). 8 cold boxes, 80kW compute, 50C output water suitable for district heating. 98% waste heat utilization rate. 100% renewable energy. 8,000 sqm facility.

PUE 1.19 (Zurich West 2), hybrid air/liquid cooling for high-density computing, 40MW from two substations, 100,000-litre cooling liquid storage tanks. Free cooling using ambient air. 100% renewable energy. Prix Watt d'Or awarded. Tier IV.

PUE 1.2 via EUR 15M river cooling system. Underground mine water at 15C year-round through 3km buried pipework. Eliminates electric chillers. Saves 18,400 MWh/year, 795 tonnes CO2. Hot water output feeds Euromediterranee urban heating (500,000 sqm). 30x more efficient than traditional cooling.

PUE 1.15 via indirect adiabatic cooling and hot-aisle containment. WUE 0.380 L/kWh. ISO 14001 certified. 100% green electricity. Greenhouse Datacenters (co-located operator) investing in liquid cooling for AI. Tier 3, N+1 redundancy.
PUE 1.2, multiple cooling technologies: direct-to-chip (up to 130kW/rack), immersion, rear door cooling. Expanding to 150MW. Security levels 3+ to 4. 140+ kW/rack density supported. AI/HPC focused.

PUE 1.1, world's first gigawatt-scale seawater cooling, integrated liquid cooling. SIN01: 26MW IT (expanded from 14MW via liquid cooling). WUE 0. 100% renewable. OCP Ready v2 certified. European Data Center Project of the Year 2025. EUR 8.5B campus investment.

address: Vila Franca de Xira, Lisbon District
notes: PUE 1.15 portfolio-wide. ThermalWorks waterless cooling: 70kW/rack (air) up to 200kW/rack (liquid cooling plug-and-play). 180MW critical power (expandable to 300MW). Zero water consumption for cooling. Saves 2.7B liters water/year vs conventional. 100% renewable. OCP Ready.

PUE 1.2 with virtually no water for cooling. Closed-loop chilled water via air-cooled chillers. 52MW IT (32MW + 20MW buildings). EUR 1B investment. On-site 100MVA multi-fuel generation (HVO renewable + gas). 22-acre campus.

LATAM's largest DC campus. Phase 2 operational with liquid cooling up to 100kW/rack. PUE 1.35. 100MW new cluster under construction for 2025. Total campus: 600MW across 17 buildings. Master plan approved. $1.13B Phase 2 investment.
DAMAC Digital (rebranded June 2025) operates hybrid cooling in Riyadh. PUE improved 1.45 to 1.37 with adiabatic+R1234ze system. Liquid immersion in portfolio. Expanding globally to 4,000MW.
KDDI containerized immersion cooling DC at MHI Yokohama Hardtech Hub. PUE below 1.07 verified. 50kVA server load, 53.5kVA total. GIGABYTE R282-Z93/R182-Z91 rack servers.
KDDI demonstration achieved PUE 1.05 with immersion cooling at Oyama Network Center. 94% reduction in cooling energy vs conventional air cooling.

facility_name: IIJ Shiroi Data Center Campus Phase 3
notes: Phase 3 building at Shiroi campus: 10MW initial (expandable to 25MW), 1,000 racks, 5,400 sqm. DLC-ready design with reserved pipe routing. Construction began June 2025, operational FY2026.

PFN/IIJ/JAIST AImod: direct liquid-cooled high-density AI servers. Design pPUE 1.1 (free cooling mode), 1.2 annual average. 30 server nodes with 256 MN-Core 2 AI processors. NEDO-funded. Test ops July 2025, full-scale April 2026.
350MW+ campus with 30,000+ racks. PUE 1.13 verified. Hybrid liquid+air cooling system developed with HEC Group. Industry standards development for cold-plate LC in China.

National Green Data Center (3 consecutive years). Best annual PUE 1.15 (2022). ~90,000 sqm. Plate liquid cooling R&D with SJTU. Building 2 PUE 1.27, WUE 0.995 L/kWh (2023). 100% green power for Building 2.

facility_name: ITCen-Aquatron Carbon Neutral AI Data Center
notes: MOU signed for carbon-neutral AI DC in South Korea. Target PUE 1.051. Captures 95% of carbon for green cement. Energy from natural gas, hydrogen, ammonia.

address: Hong Kong (Sha Tsui Road), New Territories
notes: US$124M investment. 6th HK IBX. Liquid cooling for AI workloads. PUE target 1.2 or lower. 100% renewable energy. Operational Q1 2026. Partners: Dell Technologies, Schneider Electric.

AirTrunk's first APAC deployment of direct-to-chip liquid cooling. 150MW initial (100MW expansion underway). Design PUE 1.15. 10.3 hectares. Rooftop solar with Pekat Solar. NVIDIA DGX-ready colocation partner.
Largest single campus in APAC (ex-China). 320MW+ across 9 phases, 8.3 hectares. Design PUE 1.15. 132kV onsite substation. 80% less water than traditional DC.

atNorth ICE02 Mjolnir is a Tier 3 campus on 9 hectares near Keflavik airport, Iceland. 83MW operational with 35MW expansion planned. PUE <=1.2 using free natural air cooling. All newer atNorth data centers are liquid cooling ready. 148,003 sqft raised floor. 100% renewable geothermal/hydro power.

Borealis Data Center operates in Blonduos, Iceland with PUE as low as 1.06. Direct air cooling (B52) and indirect air cooling (B62) buildings. OCP Ready certified — first in Iceland. 63MW+ grid connection expandable to 100MW+. ISO 27001 certified. 100% renewable energy. B62 has dual power feed, UPS, generators (Tier 3 equivalent).

atNorth ICE01 (formerly Thor DC) is a 3.2MW facility in Reykjavik with PUE of 1.16 after first year of operation. Uses AST Global NFC (natural free cooling) containers. Up to 30kW per rack. 100% renewable geothermal+hydro. Option to expand to 19.2MW. 2,700 sqm facility.

AQ Compute AQ-OSL1 in Honefoss is a greenfield AI-ready facility. Phase 1: 6MW IT power. Average PUE 1.05 with free cooling. Supports RDHX (rear door heat exchangers), immersion cooling, and direct-to-chip cooling. Up to 20kW/rack with free cooling, up to 80kW/rack with DLC from Cloud & Heat Technologies. Expansion adds 15MW (total 21MW), campus scalable to 171MW. Partnered with NexGen Cloud for net-zero AI Supercloud.

ASP Data Center in Stavanger offers build-to-suit facilities from 10MW to 300MW. PUE below 1.2. Liquid cooling infrastructure including direct liquid cooling, on-chip cooling, and rear-door cooling. N+1 cooling redundancy. 100% renewable hydropower. Waste heat recovery to district heating grid. Tier 3 equivalent uptime. EN 50600 compliant. Average climate 8C in SW Norway.

Bulk N01 is a massive campus in Kristiansand area spanning 3kmÂ² with up to 1GW power potential. Current 42MW facility across 4 halls (~10.5MW each). Indirect air-to-air and chilled-water cooling plus DLC support. 100+ kW/rack in advanced configurations. PUE target <1.2. 100% green hydropower from 3.6GW substation with 12 independent feeds.

Formerly Fortlax F2/F3, now EcoDataCenter Pitea. 5.3MW electrical capacity, 140 racks at up to 20kW/rack. PUE 1.1 using outside air free cooling near the polar circle. N+1 redundancy for electricity, cooling, and UPS. EcoDataCenter acquired Fortlax 2019, brand removed Oct 2020.

Nebius (formerly Yandex) Mantsala facility tripling capacity to 75MW. PUE as low as 1.1. Free cooling reduces reliance on chillers. Runs at higher server temps (up to 40C) saving ~15% energy. Heat recovery for local residential heating. 60,000 GPU capacity. Deploying NVIDIA H100, H200, and Blackwell platform (first in Europe for Blackwell). Among first European Blackwell deployments 2025.

Colt DCS London 3 (London North) in Welwyn Garden City. PUE 1.15 via distributed cooling system. 33 MVA on-site power. 8kW per rack. Tier III minimum with N+N power design. 20 miles north of London. Recently added 4 new data halls.

Colt DCS London 4 is a 31MW hyperscale facility at Hayes Digital Park. Designed for cloud and AI/HPC workloads. PUE <1.3 target. 9 data halls across 5 floors. BREEAM Very Good target. 100% renewable energy PPA. Waste heat recovery and water recycling planned. Phase 1 of larger Hayes campus expanding to 160MW (London 4, 6, 7, 8). GBP 2.5B total investment. London 6/7/8 add 97MW, construction mid-2026, L6 live early 2029.

DataVita DV1 in North Lanarkshire is Scotland's largest data center. Current 40MW capacity with 500MW+ expansion planned. Supports direct-to-chip, air, and immersive cooling. Adiabatic cooling (Scotland avg 8C year-round eliminates mechanical cooling need). Design PUE 1.18 full load; new expansion targets 1.15. Closed-loop cooling, near-zero water consumption. UK AI Growth Zone designation. 35% lower operating costs than other European hubs.

Romania's largest DC, Uptime Institute certified. PUE 1.1. NVIDIA DGX-Ready certified partner with DGX A100 installed. AIC partnership for 800MW total build-out across 2 campuses. 10MW today, 70MW by end-2026, 512MW at MSC1 alone. Massive expansion = huge cooling infrastructure opportunity.

PUE 1.1242 (TUV SUD certified -- best ever recorded by TUV SUD). Direct free cooling, 92% of all cooling via outside air. Cold-aisle containment. No water cooling, no liquid cooling. 5 data centers in Nuremberg park. Hetzner also operates Falkenstein park (PUE 1.1242 certified there too). EnEfG compliant well ahead of deadlines.

Korea's first HPC-dedicated data center. PUE 1.12 achieved via natural air cooling and liquid cooling integrated from design stage. Target PUE 1.1. Six floors, four server rooms. Launched Dec 2022. Mutually backed up across three Samsung SDS DCs.
39,780 sqm facility opened Sept 2019. PUE 1.2. Software Defined Data Center (SDDC) design. DR facility for Samsung SDS network. Low-energy modular design.

600,000+ servers. PUE 1.09 via year-round free cooling (cold climate zone). First in China to use heat-pump technology (Green Power Certificate 2021). Wind power from local farms. Part of five 'Super Data Centers'. 26,000+ tons CO2 reduction.

facility_name: Alibaba Cloud Qiandao Lake Data Center
notes: Lake water free cooling 90%+ of the time (no mechanical chillers). Water pumped through DC and returned via 2.5km canal through Qingxi New Town. Design PUE <1.3, achieved PUE 1.13. 80% energy cost reduction vs mechanical cooling. 30M kWh/year saved. WUE <0.2.

facility_name: Tencent Qingyuan Cloud Computing Data Center
notes: 270,000 sqm total planned. 8 factory buildings + 1 comprehensive building. T-block modular prefab design. PUE <1.25 in high-temp South China. 13.3 MWp rooftop solar. Leading efficiency for the region.

58MW. PUE 1.25 (most energy-efficient multi-tenant DC in tropical Singapore). Singapore's largest DTC liquid cooling deployment. 120,000 sqft, 8-storey. Integrated cable landing station. Green Mark Platinum. 90%+ pre-committed. Brings Nxera total to 120MW.

facility_name: Spark NZ Takanini Data Center (with Heat Recovery)
notes: 12.3MW (recently expanded from 2.3MW, adding 10MW). Planning another 15MW expansion. Design PUE <1.25. Heat exchange to Aventuur surfing lagoon planned. 7-hectare onsite solar farm. EPA approved. IL4 seismic rating.

67,000 sqm Sydney facility. Target PUE 1.35. Global Switch has announced full suite of LC options portfolio-wide for AI/HPC. Sydney facility uses free cooling. LC pilots underway at European and Hong Kong sites.

facility_name: Macquarie IC3 East (Macquarie Park) - Submer Immersion
notes: 11MW IT Load, 13,400 sqm. PUE 1.28. Australia's first Submer liquid immersion cooling setup via ResetData partnership. Supports both air and liquid cooling. CO2 reduction up to 45%. 90% smaller cooling footprint. Heat recyclable, no wastewater.

Nigeria's lowest PUE (1.35). 12MW AI-ready facility. Six data halls, 3,240 sqm. N+2 cooling redundancy. Hyperscale-ready. Predominantly gas power with solar planned. Commissioned April 2025. Equinix-backed (MainOne parent).

Massive 1GW sovereign data center campus in Ekibastuz. Phase 1 = four 50MW AI-focused DCs. PUE target 1.25 (Tier III). 300MW power reserved at GRES-1. Officially launched Jan 2026. AI workload focus strongly implies future liquid cooling requirements. Coal-powered but looking at green alternatives. Enormous scale = high-value cooling vendor engagement opportunity at design stage.

facility_name: Delska EU North Riga LV DC1
notes: Baltic AI data center with confirmed direct-to-chip liquid cooling readiness and 250kW/rack capability. 10MW Phase 1, expanding to 30MW. Weiss Technik Vindur CoolWall + Trane chillers. PUE target 1.3. 100% renewable energy. Uptime Tier III. LEED certification in progress. Heat recovery discussions with Riga municipality. President of Latvia visited facility. Very high-value liquid cooling vendor opportunity — facility designed for D2C from ground up.

address: Near Bucharest (15 min from Otopeni Airport), Bucharest
notes: New Romanian DC with confirmed Direct-to-Chip Liquid Cooling option at 50kW/rack. 5MW total (3MW IT) across 4 halls, 640 racks. Fan Wall cooling + N+1 free-cooling chillers. Design PUE 1.3. Tier III target. Seismic isolation for data halls. Romania accounts for 40%+ of upcoming CEE rack capacity. Small enough for cooling vendor pilot, DLC-ready from design.

facility_name: Raxio Tanzania TZ1 — Dar es Salaam
address: Dar es Salaam, Dar es Salaam
notes: Tanzania's first carrier-neutral Tier III DC. 6MW, 800 racks, indirect adiabatic cooling (PUE 1.3). IFC-backed Raxio Group ($100M commitment to African DCs). 2N power, 48hr fuel backup. 11 carriers. Under construction for 2026 launch. Hot climate = cooling is critical infrastructure. Future high-density or AI workloads could drive liquid cooling upgrade.

26.9 PFLOPS. PUE 1.05. Lenovo Neptune warm-water cooling since SuperMUC (2012). Waste heat reused for office heating and cold water generation. Blue Lion successor (2027) will be 100% DLC with 40C warm water, 30x more performance, EUR 250M. IT load approximately 3.5MW.

facility_name: China Mobile Hohhot Intelligent Computing Center
notes: World's largest single-unit liquid-cooled intelligent computing center operated by a telecom. 6.7 EFLOPS capacity, ~20,000 AI accelerator cards. Cold plate liquid cooling at scale with PUE below 1.15. Opened April 2024. Part of China's Eastern Data Western Computing strategy. Annual electricity savings of 30M kWh across buildings 7 and 9.

30MW IT load, 10,900 sqm server room. Liquid cooling installed as standard (Green Nexcenter). pPUE 1.15 target. Opening March 2026. NTT also operates a 75kW 'Data Center Trial Field' in Noda City, Chiba for liquid cooling verification (direct liquid cooling + immersion cooling devices). NTT testing two-phase direct-to-chip cooling in existing air-cooled DC.

facility_name: Deutsche Telekom T-Systems Magdeburg DC (Water-Cooled Rack Pilot)
notes: Pilot project with water-cooled racks comparing efficiency vs conventional air cooling. Part of T-Systems/Fraunhofer IFF net-zero sustainability partnership. PUE 1.3 at nearby Biere DC (PUE 1.28). Goal: net-zero off-grid data center. Analyzed 98 technologies. Potential to save 20,000 tons CO2/year. DT uses 100% renewable energy since 2021.

Vodafone DCs achieve PUE 1.2 or lower. Switching from mechanical to natural fresh air cooling systems. Large DCs in Ireland, Italy, Germany, UK. Investing over 10 years in DC migration and efficiency. 93% reduction in operational emissions since FY2020. CDP 'A List' for 2024. No confirmed liquid/immersion cooling deployment yet -- potential future adopter.

Japan's first ultra energy-efficient DC service compatible with liquid cooling technology (Green Nexcenter). pPUE 1.15 achieved. Liquid cooling as standard infrastructure. Opened fiscal 2024. Serves Tokyo metropolitan area.

facility_name: CEA DAM — French Nuclear Defense Supercomputer (EXA1 / BullSequana DLC)
notes: French nuclear defense supercomputer center. CEA/DAM operates EXA1 (TOP500 #14 at launch) with 100% DLC — Atos BullSequana XH2000. PUE ~1.0. Used for nuclear warhead modeling, submarine reactor design. XH3000 upgrade contracted Nov 2022. Atos/Eviden patented DLC is European sovereign technology — manufactured in Angers, France. One of Europe's most powerful defense HPC systems.

facility_name: Leonardo Supercomputer — CINECA Bologna Technopole (Direct Liquid Cooled)
notes: EuroHPC pre-exascale supercomputer at CINECA Bologna. 4,992 liquid-cooled nodes. 10km DLC piping. 95% heat extraction. PUE target below 1.1. Expanding from 8MW to 20MW IT load (2025-2030). Stage 2a favors 16MW DLC + 4MW air. Atos BullSequana XH2000. Italian government co-funded.

facility_name: Digital Edge NARRA1 Manila --- Nortek StatePoint Deployment
notes: First commercial colo operator to deploy StatePoint in Asia. 2,200 cabinets, 10MW IT power. Annualized PUE 1.193, WUE 1.355. Saves 35-50% on water and 20% on power. Joint venture with Threadborne Group. Won W.Media Asia Pacific award for Innovation in Energy Efficiency. Opened early 2023.

company: University of Cambridge Research Computing Services
facility_name: Dawn AI Supercomputer -- University of Cambridge
notes: UK's fastest AI supercomputer. 256 Dell PowerEdge XE9640 nodes with 512 Intel Xeon Scalable CPUs + 1,024 Intel Data Center GPU Max 1550 accelerators. Direct liquid cooling handles 60kW per rack. PUE 1.14. Phase 2 expansion with AMD MI355X GPUs funded at 36M GBP. Part of Cambridge Open Zettascale Lab.

facility_name: NVIDIA Cambridge-1 Supercomputer -- Kao Data, Harlow
notes: UK's most powerful supercomputer dedicated to healthcare AI. 80 NVIDIA DGX A100 systems (640 A100 GPUs), ~400 PFLOPS AI compute, ~10 PFLOPS Linpack. $100M+ NVIDIA investment. Hosted at Kao Data Harlow with PUE 1.2, 100% free cooling, 100% renewable energy. Founding partners: AstraZeneca (MegaMolBART drug discovery), GSK, King's College London, Oxford Nanopore. Now integrated into NVIDIA DGX Cloud for broader EU/UK access. Green500 Top 5 at launch.

company: University of Bristol / Bristol Centre for Supercomputing (BriCS)
facility_name: Isambard-AI Phase 2 -- UK National AI Research Resource (AIRR)
notes: UK sovereign AI supercomputer launched July 2025 at National Composites Centre in Bristol. 5,448 NVIDIA GH200 Grace Hopper Superchips via HPE Cray EX. 23 exaflops AI performance. 100% fan-less direct liquid cooling with PUE 1.1. All 5MW from zero-carbon grid supply. Part of UK AI Research Resource (AIRR) with 20x expansion planned by 2030. 225M GBP Phase 2 investment. Government-backed sovereign compute for UK AI research and public sector.

facility_name: NCHC Nano4 -- Taiwan National AI Supercomputer (ASUS DLC)
notes: Taiwan's sovereign AI supercomputer. NCHC Nano4 with ASUS Direct-to-Chip liquid cooling. PUE 1.18. NVIDIA HGX H200 cluster at 81.55 PFLOPS (TOP500 #29). GB200 NVL72 Blackwell phase deploying in 2026. Part of Taiwan's 'Ten Major AI Infrastructure Projects.' ASUS AIDC automated setup (3 weeks to 3 days). Highest-density AI facility in Taiwan. NVIDIA + Foxconn AI factory for TSMC also planned.

facility_name: Green Mountain DC1-Stavanger (Rennesoy) -- Fjord-Cooled with Lobster Farm + Fish Heat Reuse
notes: Green Mountain DC1-Stavanger on Rennesoy island is built inside concrete buildings within mountain caves. Gravity-fed fjord seawater cooling at 8C flows through the data center. Warmed to exactly 20C -- the ideal temperature for lobster breeding. Adjacent Hima Seafood facility uses the waste heat for world's first land-based lobster farm. Also supports fish production with stable water temperatures, reducing additional electricity consumption.

facility_name: Highlander Hainan Underwater Intelligent Computing Center
notes: World's first commercially operational underwater data center cluster. Two 1,300-tonne capsules submerged 35m underwater off Hainan Island use the South China Sea as a passive cooling system. Each capsule holds 24 server racks (up to 500 servers). Combined cluster has ~1,200 servers capable of handling 7,000 DeepSeek conversations per second. PUE 1.1 -- 30% better than China's national average of 1.5. Expansion to 100 capsules across 68,000 sqm of seabed planned within 5 years. Saves 90% of cooling energy vs. traditional DCs.

facility_name: Lefdal Mine Datacenter (Underground Olivine Mine, Fjord-Cooled)
notes: Former olivine mine 60m below sea level and 700m inside a mountain on Norway's west coast. 12 modular heat exchangers (90MW total cooling) draw 8C water from 100m depth in a 500m-deep fjord. Supports up to 300kW+ per rack via DLC and immersion cooling (designing 400kW solutions). 100% hydro and wind power. Guaranteed PUE 1.15. Near-zero WUE. Hosts Olivia -- Norway's most powerful supercomputer (HPE Cray EX, 13.2 petaflops, 64,512 CPUs + 304 GPUs, ranked #22 Green500). 225M NOK ($22.6M) system inaugurated June 2025. Expandable to 200MW and 120,000 sqm whitespace.

KDDI containerized immersion-cooled micro DC. PUE 1.05-1.07. 12ft shipping container form factor. 43% power reduction for cooling. 94% reduction in temperature control electricity vs air cooling in trials. Under 1MW -- strong SOM candidate for edge deployments.

STACK/DigiPlex OSL03 Fetsund campus. PUE 1.1 via innovative Air-to-Air cooling exploiting Norway's climate. ~10MW. Now part of STACK Infrastructure portfolio. Potential SOM candidate at 10MW boundary.
Telehouse North Two in London Docklands. Design PUE 1.16 using indirect adiabatic cooling -- first multi-floor urban DC to use this system. ~8MW. Potential SOM candidate.

facility_name: Sabey Data Centers -- Columbia Campus (Quincy, WA)
notes: Sabey Columbia campus in Quincy, WA. Average PUE 1.2. Near hydropower dams, cool/dry climate. ~10MW. Potential SOM candidate at 10MW boundary.
Kao Data KLON-01 at Harlow campus. SLA-backed PUE 1.2, consistent even at partial loads. Indirect evaporative cooling, no mechanical refrigeration. 8.8MW. Potential SOM candidate.

facility_name: EDGNEX (DAMAC) Cikarang -- 144MW AI-Optimized Data Center (Jakarta, Indonesia)
notes: EDGNEX (DAMAC) investing $2.3B in a 144MW AI-focused data center in Cikarang, Bekasi (east of Jakarta). 50,000 sqm site at Greenland International Industrial Center. PUE target 1.32. Phase 1 operational Dec 2026. One of Southeast Asia's largest AI-dedicated developments.

facility_name: STACK EMEA STO01 — Upplands Väsby, Stockholm
pue_source: CIBSE Journal peer review + Concert Control system
notes: SOM — Book Today. PUE 1.06 confirmed. Indirect evaporative air-to-air cooling (IEC) — borderline liquid scope. Waste heat feeds Stockholm district heating. Open commercial colocation. Formerly DigiPlex Stockholm.

facility_name: Digital Realty Paris Digital Park (PAR8–PAR11) — La Courneuve
address: 2 Avenue Marcel Cachin, 93120 La Courneuve, France
pue_source: Mordor Intelligence / Digital Realty disclosures — liquid zones only
notes: SAM — Engage Now. DLC (direct-to-chip) + rear-door heat exchangers commercially launched globally across 170 DLR sites May 2024 including Paris. Paris DLC-specific installation confirmed May 2023. PUE ~1.05 applies to liquid zones only, campus average is higher. Sales cycle typically 6–18 months for wholesale suites.

facility_name: Green IT Cube — GSI/FAIR Campus, Darmstadt
pue_source: GSI official KPI 2023: PUE 1.06; design PUE <1.07; acceptance test 1.051
notes: SAM — Partial Access. Rear-door water cooling (passive cold plates on rack doors) + new DLC layer (Rittal, 2025). Water cooling live since inauguration Jan 2016. PUE excellently verified at 1.06. Primarily scientific/public sector access (GSI/FAIR research). Digital Open Lab (DOL) framework allows industrial/commercial partners. hessian.AI Innovation Lab co-located. Commercial access via DOL partnership — not standard open-market colo.

address: Spain (exact sites under selection as of March 2024)
notes: SAM — 1–2 Years. Single-phase immersion (Submer SmartPod platform, 140 kW/SmartPod). 465 MW total planned across three DCs. Locations under selection. Submer Barcelona 56 MW own-operated DC announced Feb 2025 — planned for 2026. Engage with Solvenz and Submer now for first-mover position for 2026 opening.

facility_name: Asperitas Immersion Deployment — Crédit Agricole, France
address: France (exact address undisclosed — private enterprise deployment)
notes: TAM — Private / No Access. Single-phase immersion (Asperitas natural convection tanks, no fans). Private enterprise deployment for Crédit Agricole (financial sector). Not a colocation facility — no third-party access. Demonstrates immersion at 1.04 PUE in a French financial institution.

pue_source: Verified at 54 MW Airedale Frankfurt campus — Intelliscale design
notes: SAM — Engage Now. Intelliscale platform: DLC + immersion + RDHx (300 kW/rack capable). Intelliscale 300 kW/rack confirmed in Aug 2023 press release. FRA6 itself described as air-cooled UPS in facility page (liquid not confirmed at FRA6 specifically). Build-to-suit model — no standard liquid colo booking; wholesale only; requires bespoke negotiation. Engage now for 2026–2027 delivery.

pue_source: OVHcloud sustainability report — group average; best sites approach 1.20
notes: TAM — Own-Use Only. Custom direct-to-chip water cooling (proprietary since 2003, 100+ patents). OVHcloud DCs are strictly for own-use (cloud and dedicated server hosting). NOT a colocation facility — third-party tenants cannot book liquid-cooled server capacity. Largest proprietary liquid cooling deployment in Europe. R&D in Croix. Group average PUE 1.28 is above the 1.20 scope ceiling.
54MW, 6x 9MW halls. Closed-loop water cooling. Heat reuse ready. 500sqm solar. BREEAM.
4MW UNDERGROUND DC. Anti-drone/sabotage. AI compute. Qualifies on MW!

facility_name: China Mobile Harbin Intelligent Computing Center
notes: Planned companion to Hohhot center. Together with Hohhot and Guiyang centers, will deliver computing power equivalent to ~60,000 AI accelerator cards. Severely cold climate (-30C winters) ideal for free cooling supplemented by liquid cooling.

facility_name: China Mobile Guiyang Intelligent Computing Center
notes: Third node in China Mobile's national AI computing triangle (Hohhot-Harbin-Guiyang). Located in Gui'an New Area data center cluster, a key hub in China's Eastern Data Western Computing strategy. Guizhou is a major DC destination with cool highland climate and cheap hydro power.

facility_name: SK Telecom Ulsan AI Data Center
notes: Planned expansion to 1GW-scale capacity. 69,300 sqm complex described as Korea's most advanced AI-optimized data center featuring liquid cooling systems and AI-driven power management. Part of SKT's three-region AIDC strategy (Seoul metro, southern, southwestern Korea). CEO Jung Jaihun's flagship project.

World's first data center cooled by LNG cold energy. Partnership between KT and KOGAS (Korea Gas Corporation). LNG regasification releases cold at -160C. Could save ~12MW of cooling power for a mid-sized DC. 60% reduction in annual power consumption estimated. Located at KOGAS LNG import terminal at Incheon near Seoul.

facility_name: Telstra InfraCo Minchinbury AI Data Center
notes: 62MW AI-optimized data center in Western Sydney. JV with Starwood Capital Group and Doma Infrastructure Group. Liquid cooling and AI-native rack densities built into design. Carrier-neutral. Development approval secured. Construction start early 2026, RFS early Q1 2028. Telstra InfraCo also offers liquid DLC and immersion capabilities across existing portfolio.

facility_name: Samsung Electronics — Chemours Opteon Two-Phase Immersion Cooling (SSD Qualification)
notes: Samsung Electronics qualified Chemours Opteon two-phase immersion cooling fluid for Gen4 SSDs (Aug 2025). First two-phase fluid approved by Samsung. Tested in commercial 48U LiquidStack tank with PKI Corp. Eliminates water use, 60% space reduction, 40% energy reduction, 90% cooling energy reduction. Enables Samsung SSDs in immersion-cooled data centers globally.

facility_name: Samsung C&T — GRC Immersion Cooling Strategic Partnership
notes: Samsung conglomerate multi-pronged data center cooling strategy: (1) Samsung C&T E&C + GRC strategic partnership for single-phase immersion cooling (June 2025). (2) Samsung Ventures invested in GRC. (3) Samsung C&T developed own immersion cooling (80% energy reduction vs air). (4) Samsung Electronics acquired FlaktGroup (Europe's largest HVAC) for EUR 1.5B (completed Dec 2025). (5) Samsung+Chemours Opteon two-phase fluid qualification. Reference designs for AI, HPC, and Edge deployments.

$400M investment. 195,670 sq ft single-story DC at 2580 FM Road 471 N. Construction mid-Aug 2026, completion June 2028. Part of Microsoft's San Antonio metro expansion. All new MS DCs from Aug 2024 onward designed with zero-water closed-loop liquid cooling.

facility_name: Oracle/Stargate Saline Township Michigan AI Campus
notes: 1GW campus. Three 550,000 sq ft single-story buildings on 250 acres. Closed-loop cooling confirmed. Construction start early 2026, pending MI Public Service Commission approval. Walbridge as GC. DTE Energy + battery storage for power.

facility_name: Oracle/Stargate Dona Ana County New Mexico AI Campus
address: Dona Ana County (Las Cruces area), New Mexico
notes: One of five new Stargate sites announced Sep 2025. Oracle confirms direct-to-chip closed-loop non-evaporative cooling in all AI data centers including New Mexico. Desert climate makes zero-water cooling critical.

$3.8B investment announced Jan 2025 by Thailand Board of Investment. Operations expected 2026. At this scale and for AI workloads, liquid cooling is extremely likely. Tropical climate = cooling is critical design constraint.

UK's 750M GBP national supercomputer planned for Edinburgh. 31MW heat output projected. Housed in EPCC Advanced Computing Facility new wing (31M GBP). Heat recovery via old mine workings studied. No longer called exascale. Target early 2027 operational. Major sovereign compute investment for AI, science, industry. Liquid cooling virtually certain but specs not yet finalized.

facility_name: South Korea National AI Computing Center -- Samsung SDS Consortium, Haenam
notes: South Korea's flagship sovereign AI compute project. Samsung SDS consortium (Naver Cloud, KT, Kakao, Samsung Electronics, Samsung C&T). 2.5T KRW (1.7B USD) contract. 51% public / 49% private SPC structure. 15,000 GPUs by 2028, expanding to 50,000 by 2030. SoLaSeaDo Data Center Park in Haenam. Part of Korea's 50-DC / 2GW sovereign AI plan. Liquid cooling highly probable but specs not yet disclosed.

facility_name: NEOM DataVolt AI Factory -- Oxagon (Net-Zero AI Campus)
notes: 1.5GW net-zero AI factory at NEOM Oxagon. 5B USD Phase 1 (300MW operational by 2028). Red Sea seawater cooling eliminates fresh water consumption. LG Electronics advanced cooling partnership. 100% renewable energy powered. Located on Red Sea coast in NEOM's industrial/innovation zone. Multiple computing density architectures. Region's first net-zero AI factory claim.

facility_name: Stargate Norway -- Nscale/Aker/OpenAI AI Gigafactory, Narvik
notes: OpenAI's first European data center. 100,000 NVIDIA GPUs by end 2026. 230MW initial capacity (expandable to 520MW). Closed-loop D2C liquid cooling. 100% renewable hydropower. Surplus GPU heat reused for local enterprises. Kvandal near Narvik in Northern Norway. Part of OpenAI for Countries programme. Nscale + Aker partnership.

facility_name: Hemiko / OPDC Old Oak Park Royal -- UK's First DC Heat Network (9,000+ Homes, London)
notes: UK's first data center waste heat network. Hemiko named delivery partner by Old Oak and Park Royal Development Corporation (OPDC). Five phases from 2028 to 2040 delivering 95GWh/year of low-carbon heat. 9,000+ homes, businesses, and Central Middlesex Hospital. Ambient loop network collects low-temperature heat from DCs, Tube tunnels, etc. Building-level heat pumps boost temperature. GBP 63M Phase 1, GBP 600M total. 264% efficiency (vs 80-90% for gas boilers). GBP 36M government funding.
Santa Fe campus. PUE 1.7 design (cold aisle containment).
5MW. N+2 cooling. Two 600sqm computer rooms.
~19MW total Queretaro capacity.
4MW new build. Under construction.
Water-cooled chiller plant + DX CRAC. 2N redundancy.
Air-cooled chillers + CRAC/CRAH. 5.5kVA/cabinet. N+1 cooling.

31MW. Tier III.

300MW total (72MW Phase 1). Delta Cube air cooling + liquid cooling ready. $3B investment. 5 buildings, 275k sqm.
37.5MW. 90416 sqft. $160M USD. ICREA Level 5 HSHA certified.
50MW edge+core campus. 5MW edge sheds for OTT caching. Land secured.

facility_name: Vertiv Mexico Manufacturing -- AI Liquid Cooling CDUs
notes: Manufacturing facility for AI liquid cooling CDUs and rear-door heat exchangers. Supply chain node, not colocation.
2.5MW. KIO Green indirect free cooling. Design PUE 1.7. Since 2007.

32MW total (8.4MW Phase 1). VYNMSA Industrial Park. Since 2022.
30MW. Amexhe Industrial Park. Q2 2024.
24MW (12MW Phase 1). Delta Cube air + closed-loop water. Liquid-ready. 50kW/rack.
900MW planned. 52 hectares. $4.8B. 200MW power secured. Waterless cooling. LEED Gold. Operations ~2027.
7.9MW. PUE <1.4. WUE zero (no water for cooling). $80M. Since Q4 2023.
6MW (3MW IT). PUE 1.3 target. Tier IV design. 3N Trane chillers. 20kW/rack.
250MW. 350MW nat gas plant. 160km pipeline. $2.7-3.7B. Fully operational 2028.

Chilled-water + free-cooling. Oracle Cloud Monterrey Region host.
